_selected_target_schema: When populated by Orchestrator: {field_a, field_c, bridge_concepts, strategy, disjointness_status, impact_potential, impact_type}
session_meta_insights: anomaly_hunting strategy (first primary session): DISJOINT target quality 8.25, produced 3 CONDITIONAL_PASS (0 full PASS). QG mean 6.97. Comparable to structural_isomorphism in pass+cond rate.

Mathematical isomorphism bridge (Markov eigendecomposition shared by Mpemba and amyloid MSM formalisms): zero PubMed co-occurrence confirmed. High-yield bridge type when both fields independently derived same formalism.
Kill pattern: fabricated citations recurred (Avanzini 2026 unverifiable; Kusumoto 1998 wrong conclusion). Generator claim-level citation verification must be stricter.

Kill pattern: IDP MSM infeasibility. Hypotheses proposing MSM construction for intrinsically disordered proteins (Abeta42 monomer, tau-K18) fail computationally. Restrict MSM hypotheses to partially-folded or folded proteins.

Kill pattern: detailed-balance MSM eliminates non-normality. Standard PyEMMA/MSMBuilder construction enforces detailed balance; non-normal Liouvillian hypothesis is untestable with any existing published MSM tool.
Success pattern: experimental anchor in measured datum (Yu et al. 2015 D_misfold/D_fold = 10^-3 for PrP under force) produces resilient hypotheses.
Success pattern: three-arm mechanism-discriminating experimental designs produce qualitatively different outputs per arm. QG flagged this as best-design feature in session.
Creativity metrics: disciplinary distance 3.0/3.0 (statistical physics x protein biochemistry). Abstraction level 2.3/3.0. Novelty type 3.0/4.0 (new framework connecting two bodies of theory).
Post-QG validation (cross-model, convergence, dataset evidence) PENDING at session report time.

computational_readiness.foundation_paper: PNAS 2017 PMID:28461467 — Markovian Mpemba effect eigenmode overlap
key_concerns: PNAS 2017 provides math framework — cite and differentiate protein MSM application
D_misfold/D_fold is inferred (plausible at ~3.3 kT roughness), not directly measured
Aggregation MSMs require new combined folding+misfolding construction
Mpemba condition requires specific eigenmode computation, not simple kinetic argument
Non-normal Liouvillian: specify irreversible aggregation regime
generator_advisories: Primary prediction: native Aβ42 aggregates faster than stressed variant via low slow-eigenmode overlap
Present D ratio as 'order-of-magnitude or greater', not exact 1000x
Use Aβ42 as primary test case — best published MSM data

key_findings: C2-H3 KILLED: evolutionary folding-aggregation tradeoff is established; Mpemba framing adds label not mechanism; IDP category error; Tartaglia citation fabricated (JMB 372:229 does not exist)
C2-H6 KILLED: tau-K18 IDP MSM computationally prohibitive (500 us MD); single-site phosphorylation below MSM noise floor; clinical extrapolation spans 15+ orders of magnitude
C2-H8 KILLED: biological mechanism (Hsp70 binds APRs) established since 1997; Mpemba framing is metaphorical rebranding not mechanistic insight
Monomer-to-aggregation gap remains Achilles heel across surviving hypotheses
C2-H7 emerges as strongest hypothesis (8.45 composite) — clean citations, three-arm mechanism discriminant, immediate experimental executability
All top-4 cycle 2 hypotheses exceed 7.0 threshold — strongest cycle 2 cohort in session

ranked_hypotheses.title: Rough Energy Landscape Diffusion Asymmetry (D_fold >> D_misfold) Creates Mpemba-Detectable Spectral Signature
Inverse Mpemba Protocol Suppresses Fibril Formation by Exploiting Eigenmode Decoupling
Mpemba Index of Protein Folding MSMs Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Propensity
Mpemba Eigenmode Branching Explains Prion Strain Selection

critic_questions.question: Has ANY published MSM analysis shown that psi_2 (the Fiedler vector / second eigenvector) separates native-like from aggregation-prone conformations in Ab42 or any IDP? If the answer is no, this must be acknowledged as the single most important unverified assumption in the hypothesis.

The monomer-to-aggregation gap remains unbridged. Can the hypothesis be explicitly reformulated to predict MISFOLDING PROPENSITY (single-molecule property) rather than AGGREGATION RATE (multi-molecule property)?

The D_misfold/D_fold ratio was measured for PrP DIMERS using single-molecule force spectroscopy. PrP has a well-defined native fold; Ab42 and alpha-synuclein are IDPs. What is the expected D ratio for an IDP that has no native folding funnel? Is D_fold even defined for an IDP?

Provide evidence that monomer A* conformational states determine fibril polymorph identity. Chakraborty 2020 shows A* predicts aggregation RATE, not polymorph IDENTITY. Without this evidence, the eigenmode-branching mechanism is unfounded.

The resource-theoretic framework requires Markovian closed-system dynamics. Protein folding in cells is an open system with ATP-dependent chaperones. Can the framework be extended to open systems, or must it be restricted to in vitro conditions?

The monomer-to-aggregation gap remains the Achilles heel of the entire hypothesis family. Secondary nucleation (Cohen 2013) dominates Ab42 proliferation and is not captured by single-molecule MSM eigenspectra. Can cycle 2 survivors explicitly address this by restricting claims to monomer misfolding propensity rather than aggregation rate?

IDP Abeta42 high-|v_slow| states likely lack binding pockets
Boltzmann reweighting diverges for strong binders (Kd < 10 uM)
Signal-to-noise: global sigma(t) may obscure misfolding-specific spike
Prigogine near-equilibrium regime assumption not justified from common 400K quench
MSM lag time sets resolution floor for spike detection
Cohen et al. citation persistent error (2012/109:9761 vs 2013/110:9758) — uncorrected from cycle 1
Yu et al. measured PrP dimers under force, not Abeta42 in solution — transfer assumption unverified
Insulin fibril polymorphism may be inherently stochastic — deterministic eigenmode prediction could fail
Dense eigenvalue spectrum may not resolve v_2 vs v_3 clearly
Insulin B-chain MSM may not capture full hormone behavior
